chat.title: AI Assistant
chat.welcome: Hello! I'm your wildfire assistant. Ask me about fire behavior, risks, or evacuation recommendations.
chat.error: Sorry, I couldn't process your request. Please try again.

tooltips.dataSource: Demo uses static weather values (wind=12km/h, 135°, 29°C, 34% humidity). Live fetches real-time weather from Open-Meteo API for your location.
tooltips.windSpeed: Wind speed in km/h. Higher values cause faster fire spread in the downwind direction and increase spotting distance.
tooltips.windDir: Direction the wind is blowing FROM (0°=North, 90°=East, 180°=South, 270°=West). Fire spreads fastest downwind.
tooltips.humidity: Relative humidity percentage. Lower humidity dries out vegetation fuel, making it more flammable and increasing fire intensity.
tooltips.temp: Temperature in Celsius. Higher temperatures pre-heat fuel ahead of the fire front, accelerating spread rates.
tooltips.apply: Manually override weather parameters for the simulation. This disables live updates and uses your custom values instead.
tooltips.tick: Advance the simulation by one time step. Fire spread is calculated based on fuel type, weather, and current fire perimeter.
tooltips.reset: Extinguish all active fires and clear the entire fire mask. The fuel map and grid remain unchanged.
tooltips.stepBtn: Manually advance the simulation by one tick. Useful for step-by-step analysis of fire spread behavior.
tooltips.clearBtn: Reset the simulation: extinguishes all fires and clears the fire mask. The fuel map remains unchanged.
tooltips.burning: Number of grid cells currently on fire. These cells will spread fire to neighboring unburned cells.
tooltips.burned: Number of grid cells that have been completely burned. Burned cells can no longer ignite.
tooltips.burnedPct: Percentage of the total 64x64 grid (4096 cells) that has been burned.
tooltips.activeFronts: Number of distinct fire perimeter segments actively spreading fire to adjacent fuel.
tooltips.legend: Color-coded legend for the simulation grid. Forest, grass, water, town, road, and firebreak fuel types with burning/burned cell states.
tooltips.grid: 64x64 simulation grid over a real-world map. Click any cell to ignite a fire. Fire spreads automatically based on fuel and weather conditions.
tooltips.gridSize: The simulation grid is fixed at 64x64 cells. Each cell represents approximately 1km² covering the San Francisco Bay Area.
tooltips.scenario: Save and load simulation scenarios. Save captures the current fuel map, fire state, and weather for later replay.
tooltips.timeline: Drag the scrubber or use arrow buttons to replay the simulation step-by-step. Each step represents one fire spread iteration.
tooltips.chat: Ask the AI assistant questions about wildfire behavior, weather effects, fire spread mechanics, evacuation strategies, or simulation controls.
